data: With a common vision, we developed a brand that is reflective of Revogen's innovative and high-quality products that help patients heal.
Made possible by the people thinking of other's need at the time of their loss

We've enjoyed a lengthy partnership with SummitTrek and consider it an honor to create tools for their coaching practice that bring lasting impact in the lives and businesses of their clients. We help communicate on their behalf by way of brand re-design, monthly workbooks, email newsletters, assorted print materials and websites. Summit is in the business of transforming and restoring lives and businesses to their original intended purpose and we love helping them do that.

Summit makes use of carefully curated quotes throughout their materials. When we recently re-designed their brand we made sure to identify a font that felt authentically hand written, to give the personal touch whenever short quotes are displayed.

We've created a comprehensive system of tools, worksheets, workbooks all with a simple minimalistic aesthetic. We never want the design to get in the way of the process or the meaningful exercises the user is experiencing.
One of the most impactful Summit offerings is the Life Planning Retreat. The weekend is an opportunity to Rekindle Your Passions. Discover Your Purpose and Live Differently.
our process is found in restoring leaders and organizations to their original intened purpose through coaching

Medical Solutions of Texas is a Service Disabled Veteran Owned Business (SDVOSB) that acts as the bridge between medical supply companies and the Department of Defense and VA hospitals. They have the connections and experience to help private companies sell their medical suplies and solutions to the government.

MSOT's lacked a brand, identity and website that looked legitimate in the eyes of the medical community or the military end user. So, we developed a brand that was inspired by stenciled and angular military lettering and logistics shipping containers. The brand uses a color palette sampled from camoflauge and carries with it that tactical "we get it done" mentality.
The lettering is distinctive, simple and no nonsense.

The MSOT website is a place for medical supply sales reps to understand how to sell to the government. Pages help the view understand how MSOT helps clarify a complex process. We featured "end users." The ultimate recipients of the  medical care offered and featured them over the lifetime of military service from new recruits, to growing families all the way up to retired and aging combat veterans.
Our websites are always designed and programmed with desktop, laptop, tablet and mobile experiences in mind.
putting while someone else holds a branded flag

After a life-changing experiencing at a Youth Peace Camp in Serbia, founder Shane Hanson knew his purpose was to create a non-profit that served Foster Youth through sports, mentorship, and training. Due to the deeply flawed foster care system,  many children who need safe and secure environments are left with a lack of structure and resources. Freedom Youth Foundation was created to help fill that gap along side a community of amazing volunteers and sponsors. They care for thousands of foster kids each year in California, Idaho and Mexico.
With a brand that has good standing within the communities they serve, it was important to honor the intent of FYF's original logo.
Once a concise look and feel was created, we quickly developed all collateral for their day-to-day operations, marketing, and events.
In a recent fundraising event, we donated our services to create branded and marketing materials for a golf tournament.
branded signs for each golf hole
hero image of putting with flag
Off-the-shelf solutions weren't up to par for the elevated aesthetic of The Burbank Portfolio.
Returning to the office in the midst of COVID was difficult for everyone. The challenge for Worthe, and its properties within The Burbank Portfolio, was maintaining a first class experience.

When you google "COVID signage" and "Social Distancing Signage", you will quickly realize that isn't really sophisticated solutions that will match a high-end office environment. Worthe came to us to help create a system of custom temporary signage as well as a "Return to Work" playbook for tenants. Both deliverables needed to fit the brand we created for The Burbank Portfolio and communicate the commitment Worthe was making to their tenants.
A playbook for all 13 properties within The Burbank Portfolio. Subject matter included commitments to safety, tenant recommendations for a phased return plan.

All 13 Burbank Portfolio buildings required safety signage throughout the interior and exterior. Our design goal was to communicate clearly without gimmicks and develop a flexible system that would work in hundreds of specific scenarios throughout all properties.
When you look for a quick fix solution online, here's is what you find.
google search of loud, unispired signage
It was an easy decision for their team.
clean, designed, black and white covid signage
“We work hard and smart to deliver excellence to our clients.”
This system needed to be done quickly while still being thoughtful and flexible. We love a good challenge.
The Burbank Portfolio properties have a number of private health clubs available for tenant use. Custom social "fitnessing" signage was needed throughout.
7600 Broadway is the flagship property for Embrey Partners. It is home to premier office space and over 200 refined residences.
Embrey is in the business of “Creating Places Where People Want to Be.”

Embrey Partners is one of the nation's premier multi-family home builders. So when they broke ground on their corporate HQ, premier offices and refined living residences in the heart of San Antonio, they needed to put their best foot forward.

A hard cover and soft cover portfolio book was created to showcase the 53,000 s.f. of premier office space. These were created to reflect the quality of the space itself and given away to potential tenants for the space.

As the property was being finished, leasing began. In order to give prospective tenants an idea of the amenities and floor plans available, we created a system of floor plans and unit plans for the management leasing team, all based off of the architectural drawings.
Marketing a new state-of-the art animal hospital for some of the most beloved members of the family... pets.
Herbst Veterinary Hospital believes in treating every animal like their own pet. So, when they outgrew their original facility, they built an amazing veterinary hospital just up the road.

The challenge was to communicate to their thousands of pet owning clients who range in age and communication preference that they'd moved. Pet owners might only see their vet a couple times per year, so we deployed a three-pronged approach to provide the most effective communication possible: printed materials, social media and email campaigns. 

It was really important to Herbst that their customers received best care possible, including communicating early and often about how their new facility can serve them even more effectively.
Social medial campaign was developed to post several times per week, leading up to the official move.
A banner was created for their old facility that let anyone know that their new facility was right up the road.
A baseball training center focussed on character, integrity, and grit.

Baseball Collab is a training and character development center in Fort Collins Colorado. Our brief: Create a unique mark that communicates their desire to be more than just a baseball training center—a place that values building up the whole player to face life with integrity and grit. Let's play ball.

Iteration and attention to detail was the key to the success of this mark. We were consistently looking for ways to simplify and refine the design to communicate quickly and clearly. The result, well, we loved it.
More than just a baseball brand
Gear that athletes actually want to wear

Why Design a t shirt that no ones wants to wear? We worked closely with the CEO vetting ideas for shirts that we were both excited about—taking inspiration from the local area code, and brand mission to produce some eye catching merch.
man wearing tank top with logo

We work hard and smart to deliver excellence to our clients. This budget was tight but the results still had to be clear and compelling. The open honest communication from the client was key to this success.

Greatness on and
off the field
This is a brand that has a mission beyond just selling merch—they have a passionate mission to develop athletes on and off the field.
logo stictched into inside of shirt neck
An elementary school with a simple mission: to build up the next generation of education.
From business cards to signage, we worked together to balance outlets of creativity while maintaining function and sophistication in the school environment.

For over 50 years, St. James’, a private elementary school located in Los Angeles, has been fostering a new generation of leaders that graduate with more than what they learned in the classroom. Our job was to develop a consistent visual language that spoke to the core of what it means to belong at St. James’. With such a vast array of touch points to be tackled, it allowed for each project to shine with a bit of its own personality.

Creating an inspiring space  was as important as a safe and welcoming environment for St. James’. Throughout the school we’ve created moments of inspiration that a derived from the school’s four pillars: wonder of childhood, academics, community, and spirituality.
love others and yourself wall sign
what do you think wall sign
Brand elements and colors used in the environment are consistently applied to printed materials for both parents and students.
A Fresh Face for the School Year
Launching soon, the new St. James’ website prioritizes the success of students by focusing on their four pillars to guide current and prospective families.
When like-minded people unite they're able to achieve more together than they could alone.

The “Guild Collective” is loosely defined as: 
A group of people, artisans or craftsmen who maintain high standards in the pursuit of a common goal.

The Guild Collective was formed in 2020 with a vision to unite skilled craftsmen who build things that last and improve the lives of their customers. There is an intentionality to their methods, their work and their customer service. They're elevating the importance of job well done and simultaneously instilling job satisfaction and meaning into their work force. It is a privilege to partner with companies like the Guild Collective who cast a bigger vision for what their business can be. Here at Reddoor, we've been inspired by the Guild's ethos. The business is actually a unification 3 different and independently successful entities. The formation of The Guild Collective was completed in the middle of the covid pandemic, and in spite of the challenges they've faced, they continue to grow and are in high demand in the Texas Hill Country. 

Our engagement with The Guild Collective include: naming, story development, branding, apparel, ID system, website and marketing brochures. One of the most important criteria for success was ”an enthusiastic adoption of the brand by the team.”
wide shot of hub street corner
Catching your eye as you stroll around the block.

Catching sun in beautiful Santa Monica, Uppababy's new HUB was ready for their customers to stroll right in. The problem? It was difficult to notice their location or find their designated customer parking.

After visiting the site, we came up with a simple plan to help navigate customers right to their door. For those walking up, we placed a sign on a column facing the street, and an opaque vinyl to their door. On the south facing wall of the building, we placed a larger sign that can easily be spotted as cars drive by or pull up into the driveway. By placing signage in these location, customers will no longer feel lost reaching their destination.
We work hard and smart to deliver excellence to our clients.
We diligently reviewed all possible options so customers can have a positive experience from the moment they pull up to when they receive high quality service.
drawn image of a craft knife
We extended the sign program to the parking area so customers can easily find their parking spots.
A production studio for healthcare strategy.
With great success, Strategy Advantage has grown into a forward thinking studio in the healthcare industry. They needed a brand to match.

Strategy Advantage acts as partners for healthcare leaders who need help navigating through the challenges of their industry. The new brand was developed to reflect the process in which they take their clients from disarray and chaos, to calm and effective solutions. To visually communicate navigating their clients from chaos to calm, or dark to light all with sense of balance we take you behind the scenes a bit and show you the creative process that makes that a reality.
more logo sketches with refined marks to the right
refined marks in different color schemes
As guides to healthcare professionals, Strategy Advantages logo was developed with the journey they provide towards innovative healthcare solutions in mind.
The best brands have a story worth telling
They deserve to be recognized, so we arm them with clear stories and compelling design that allows them to stand out and succeed.
Following a StoryBrand model, we collaborated to create their story and paired it with clear and compelling design.
The process is integral to Strategy Advantage's business. Their website reflects that through scrolling elements that lead into each page.
Custom animated infographics to help communication the value proposition that Strategy Advantage offers their clients.
With a team of professionals on hand, materials for conferences and meetings are consistently branding for Strategy Advantage.
SCFAI is a cutting-edge clinical care and research center that is revolutionizing food allergy treatment.

To better inform their patients, staff, and potential hires, we worked with Southern California Food Allergy Institute to clarify their process and develop a compelling website to clearly guide and inform visitors about their Tolerance Induction Program (TIP).

SCFAI is a forward thinking research based clinic treating children with anaphylactic allergies to achieve tolerance and remission. They take pride in the successes of their patients and are poised to grow to continue treating more children with allergies. While the TIP experience can seem daunting for young children, the life-saving experience is sought after by thousands of prospective patients. The website needed to clarify the process and invite visitors to learn more about SCFAI in a welcoming way.
To create an approachable atmosphere for patients, staff, and prospective hires, we developed a clear language using illustrations and animations throughout the site.

We dove into SCFAI's TIP program to address the confusion patients and employees were experiencing in learning more about the program. After a thorough assessment, we found key areas to highlight and so prospective and current patients can better understand how the process works, and feel confident in their treatment plan.
Using elements from their existing brand and drawing inspiration from their process, we developed an illustration style that served as the base for the icons and animations throughout the site.

One of the endearing methods for providing dosages to children is with gummy bears. We used this character to represent the patient journey by placing it in context to travel and exploration. As visitors navigate the site, the gummy bear makes it's way towards the top of the mountain, representing remission after it overcame challenges along the way.
Guiding Patients to the Right Path

With patients coming from all over the world, travel becomes a through-line in all the pages, Visitors are directed to their "zones" where they can find the appropriate information for their patient journey.
With such a playful visual direction, it was only appropriate to include special moments throughout the sites by animating illustrations to elevate the message in each page.
We are enemies of mediocrity and are unwilling to accept average work.
To create a compelling experience, we used playful elements to make the new SCFAI website approachable and informative.
website on a variety of devices

It is important for patients to understand the commitment necessary to be part of the program so the entire flow of the site is dedicated to a patient's journey. It begins by leading them to the correct treatment zone, to understanding what it means to be part of the program, and guiding them through each step within the program. With clarity, prospective and current patients can feel confident moving forward.
Premier office space among the biggest names in entertainment.

Worthe Real Estate Group and Blackstone co-own a collection of thirteen office buildings in Burbank that total over 3,000,000 s.f. of office space. They have six unique brands, one of which is a 19-acre campus with it's own state of art movie screening theater. Our unique opportunity was to unify and market the portfolio of properties as a single collection named: The Burbank Portfolio.

The challenge was to create one voice for this collection of branded properties and market the city of Burbank to companies that want to be in the center of it all for their commercial real estate needs. As we dug in, it became apparent that Burbank was and is "the cradle of content creation" and some would arguably say is the entertainment capital of the world. Our first deliverable was a video that showcased The Burbank Portfolio and the city itself.
We developed the brand, video graphics and partnered with Aether Films on an incredibly short one-month timeline.
burbank overlook with "the burbank portfolio" and buildings labeled
The Burbank Portfolio: Premier office space among the biggest names in entertainment
The amazing team at Aether Films handled the video production. Photos: Cliff William

Each distinctive property within the Burbank Portfolio has it's own brand identity developed at some point over the last 40 years. Consequently, the brands don't work together as a visual system or side-by-side in a portfolio of properties under singular ownership. We created cohesion with a simplified type system and used color to help create distinction between each property.
flyers for different buildings within design system
The main website showcases all the properties and links to individual property sites for the purpose of marketing lease renewals.
Brands have a story worth telling
With a vast portfolio encompassing all of Los Angeles County, we focused on clarifying The Burbank Portfolio story and show off each property's character within a well-designed system.
specific website spreads for each site
Each property has individual marketing needs. Our brochure system positions each building in a fresh way and highlights the specific amenities of each.
"Come be a part of it." page
women in gym with big poster of lats on the wall
Branding, signage, super graphics and a website for the portfolio of health clubs at specific properties.
FIT media studio etched in glass
bikes in front of bike on the wall
male bodybuilder poster on wall in gym
A law school equipping students to fight for justice - superhero cape and tights not included.

It seems like just about every client wants to stand out. Afterall, isn't that the point of marketing? To rise above the crowd so your potential customers will take notice? Trinity Law School wanted the same thing, except what caught our attention is that they had a purpose behind that desire... a greater purpose. They want to be more than an average law school, there are hundreds of those. They want potential students to see that they are a distinctively Christian law school helping their students fulfill their calling. The call to justice in a world filled with injustices, whether that's a social injustice, human trafficking, religious injustices, persecution of faith or domestic injustices.

We wanted potential students to stop and take notice. This oversized brochure, or view book, is anything but a stuffy conservative Christian publication. We wanted the content to be bold yet informative. Simple clear communication seems to be rare in academia, the norm tends to be: how much can we fit on a page. The team at Trinity were a huge support in removing superfluous content that got in the way of our goal - communicate what is Trinity about pave the way for easy application into the program.
A large auto company having a vehicle launch feels like inspired luxury and performance.

Press and media members are always getting invited to preview the next new automobile brands and models. How does one event stand out from the next? The design brief for the Q50 Media Preview Kit was to speak to the "inspired" way of life and the quality of the Infiniti brand. Elegant modern luxury with a focus on performance. Working with the creative team at George P. Johnson, we decide to avoid the typical glossy image brochure and cheesy branded notebook. We wanted to create something memorable and something to actually be kept and used.
several catalogs next to each other

It started with a beautiful notebook with an "Inspired" black imprint. This was accompanied by a simple silver paper folder with a hand-done blind emboss that contained the route book and trim guide for the automobile that they would be driving. The resulting kit was accompanied by other thoughtful and luxurious touches that were representative of the Infiniti Q50.
close up of trim level guide
full size open page of catalog
side on of headlight of toyota
Can the company who brought us the Prius hang in the after-market race culture of SEMA?

Every year Toyota arrives at the SEMA Show to showcase their premier automobiles. To accentuate their vehicles and truly evoke the spirit of Toyota, we created banner graphics to communicate their theme and message year by year.

For SEMA 2018, "Let's Go Places" and a brand refresh was the primary message. A clean and simplified brand that evolved beyond the metallicized Toyota logo to a graphic and flat red, white and black brand. Working with a worldwide brand and being trusted with introducing the SEMA community with new branding is a distinct privilege.
For SEMA 2017, the Power of You was the central focus of Toyota's message. New design, new aftermarket modifications and as a result of vehicle customization, a whole new driving experience.
SEMA 2016 pays homage to the trail blazing past of Toyota and looking to the exciting future. Showcase vehicles spanned Toyota's history and showcased forward-thinking concept vehicles.
SEMA 2015 was a collection of places people adventurously explore in their Toyota vehicles.
A real estate team aiming to double its business during a recession - mission accomplished.

Develop a brand from the ground up for a real estate team that needed to compete in the luxury market of a down economy.

We created a brand collaborating with the founder, James Anderson, that delicately balanced the worlds of beach casual and the luxury of California's Pacific Palisades and surrounding communities. We broke the traditional rules of real estate marketing trends, and made sure every marketing piece made a statement and was used with purpose. We created a complete system, from headline driven yard signs to business cards that have handmade quality to custom listing kits that delivered a solid first impression.

The brand was so unique and successful that it not only created 27 million dollars worth of sales in the first year and 49 million the second, but also set an industry standard bringing about guest speaking engagements, luxury marketing panel discussions and industry marketing awards. It also propelled the team into certification as a Luxury Marketing Specialist by the CLHMS and acceptance as a member of the Institute for Luxury Home Marketing.
Letterpress craftsmanship from the amazing folks at Studio on Fire.
Customized and prepared for potential clients. These were the first taste of Pacific Luxury Group's beautiful marketing and attention to detail.
A biotech company with a family of brands, all vying for attention.

Working with George P. Johnson, we tackled a massive project: take the existing global trade show properties for Life Technologies, a medical supergiant, and create guidelines for the world to use...

Life Techonlogies is comprised of more than eight internal business units on multiple continents. The solution ranged from table top graphics to a 40 x 60' exhibit and everything in-between. Along side of GPJ's Creative Director Aaron Miller, we developed new signage properties and identification fins to supplement their system allowing the individual brands to have a voice. The final deliverable was a PDF style guide that was distributed to the business units in the Americas, EMEA, and Asia-Pacific.
selection of dna and packaging images
two mechanics working on a car
Your dad's spark plugs are good enough for you, in fact they're the best.

A great collaboration with the creative team at Innocean USA, the Champion project began by updating packaging with a new design system that could be applied across a growing range of automotive and lifestyle products.

The objective was to create best-in-class packaging that would allow Champion to win back the faith of auto part retailers, and provide convincing reasons to stock the brand. "There's a Champion in Here" is a celebration of those who have an irrepressible drive to win at life. 

At more than 100-years-old, Champion is a brand with legitimate heritage in motorsports. We took inspiration from that legacy in performance automotive culture and created a simple color scheme and a graphic illustration style that makes the brand immediately recognizable. We arrived at a contemporary look for the brand that is rooted in history and dependability.

The Champion Motor Speedway, M1 Concourse in Pontiac Michigan is an 87-acre playground for auto enthusiasts that includes 250+ Private Garages and a state-of-the-art 1.5-mile Performance Track. M1 is a place where auto enthusiasts can keep, show and exercise their vehicles in the company of other enthusiasts. M1 Concourse will become the epicenter of all things automotive in metro Detroit and provide the largest concentration of auto enthusiasts in the world a venue to enjoy their passion with friends and family.
2d shape of runner over football field
Squeezing out every drop of potential by paying attention to the smallest details in sports performance.
We developed a brand, digital presentations and other identity collateral for RM Sports, a consulting agency for elite athletic teams.

RM Sports prides itself in "taking the guesswork out of high performance." In order to do so they focus on: training the trainers and coaching the coaches; providing supplemental expertise; measuring and facilitating competitive intelligence; augmenting performance development with the sport sciences; and teaching concrete techniques, skills and strategies.

Our challenge was to create a brand that felt at home in the competitive sports arena, that complemented the technical and detail-oriented consulting service, while speaking clearly to a wide audience. Our target demographic included NCAA Athletic Directors, NCAA coaches, all the way down to the athletes themselves.
The logo tells the story of how hunting for the smallest opportunities for improvement and efficiency can literally "change the game."
The logo was just the beginning for RM Sports. We continued into their Performance Reports and developed clear and compelling infographs to tell their story.
An artist enriching lives with beauty through world-renowned figurative sculpture.
"Presentation is everything." This phrase is uttered in many art schools. Your concept or execution may be flawless but if you have a lousy presentation, then what you created falls flat.

The world-renowned sculptor, Richard MacDonald, knew this all too well and felt he had neglected his website presentation far too long. We meticulously went through his past site and noted its successes and failures while working side-by-side with the artist and his team to create a rich experience of his three dimensional work online.

The obvious qualities were on the team's checklist: works well on all devices, attention grabbing homepage, easy-to-manage content and built for SEO. We didn't want the museum like experience of the art to be lost. Being able to see his sculpture in person is something magical. It's much more challenging to make the sculpture come alive on a two-dimensional medium. We had to get two things right: the incredible detail of the work and the three dimensional experience. This is a tough nut to crack since we are dealing with multiple devices, many screen sizes and functionality.

With the help of designer/developer, Thom Meredith, we did this in two ways. The first is a powerful yet simple homepage sildeshow that shows macroview croppings of the artwork texture. The interesting twist with this sildeshow is that we ensured that we had a different collection of images, depending on whether the device was in horizontal or vertical orientation.

The second was the three-dimensional experience. The technology in this realm continues to improve and we were able to implement a 360 degree view of the artwork that user could control with their mouse or finger on touch devices. This sounds simple and obvious but a few years ago the only options were crude and didn't work on touch devices.
Creating a great experience for mobile devices using the same image database was critical.
We place a primary emphasis on our client's needs.
Looking beyond the website, we continued to develop other areas of presentation to properly represent these sculptures in printed pieces and exhibit spaces.

A catalogue raisonné was created for the exhibition celebrating the first 25 years of Richard MacDonald's sculpture. This print piece was awarded a regional and national print award, in partnership with the fine printing team at Dual Graphics.

It is tempting to overuse the word 'energy' when discussing Richard MacDonald. The man himself, his work, the indomitable challenges he has stared down in the course of his career, his models, his subject matter—the very material of bronze itself, emerging from the fiery crucible, glows with energy. The first twenty five years of Richard MacDonald's crusade to reimagine figurative sculpture for our time is a story of will, focus, and relentless, unstoppable movement forward.

The Exhibition is an attempt to the display the artist's achievements while foreshadowing the future. Reddoor helped develop the concept of the exhibition while creating all exhibition graphics and customizing them to multiple gallery installations. From large image panels down to small name plaques for the individual pieces, this is a complete branded traveling exhibition. It wouldn't have been possible without the creative energy and vision of our client.

Stationery systems can be dull and ordinary or they can be a beautiful expression of the entity they represent. In this case we strove for the second, designing a system that supports the world-renowned sculptor, Richard MacDonald. His art is dimensional and expressive, while Richard MacDonald Studios is a professional corporation, the stationery needed to speak to quality of the art it represented.

The large blind-debossed "Richard MacDonald" was a connection to the dimensional work that sometimes can be as large as 26 feet tall. The supporting type was engraved, the highest quality printing available, representing the quality of the organization that supports the artist.

To say that the art of Richard MacDonald is powerful is a bit of understatement. As one of the world's leading figurative sculptors, his art has an emotive quality that can only really be experienced in person. So when Richard MacDonald Studios came to us with the challenge of conveying that experience in a two dimensional advertisement we jumped at the opportunity. Ultimately, we presented the art in a dramatic fashion, on a stark black background with a simple tagline that supported the experience when viewing the art in person.
skateboard on top of a bike on a beach
"Wouldn't it be cool to own a skateboard company, design all of the deck art and ride one to work?" We did.

A retro skate brand for all ages, based in Southern California, that produced classic and progressive long board skates. Ashland produced a select range of boards from beach cruising long boards all the way down to retro 80's pool boards.

A retro logo and color palette that feels at home today or in 1976. Overall the branding was meant to be fun & irreverent. Two-color sticker packs were created to further the brand awareness in the marketplace.
logo and logomark in several colors
woman in black and white smiling
A non-profit keeping the heartbeat of Texas thriving for generations to come.

The Texas Organ Sharing Alliance (TOSA) saves lives through the power of organ donation. 

The TOSA logo has significant meaning and relates directly to the life-saving work they're committed to. The heart represents warmth, life and generosity. The heart shape blends into a partial infinity symbol, representing the continuation of life via organ donation. The infinity symbol alone can be read as the number eight, which is the number of lives a single organ donor can save. The blue-to-red color gradation represents how the loss of life brings about new life.
writing to your donor family pamphlet
many many sheets of the procedure
A mission to create beautiful and affordable lighting for the home

Progress lighting is one of the nation's largest and oldest producers and manufacturers of residential lighting solutions. Being a company that works in such high volume, it would easy to sacrifice beauty for utility. But with a stellar in-house product design team and partnering with an Elle Decor Top 100 designer, you'll see that's not the case. That's really the story here for us, with catalogs of hundreds of pages and thousands of images, how do you not let that immense task get in the way of good design.

Being details and logistics focused, we've found ways to organize and communicate their brand in elegant ways. Whether it's a nearly one-thousand page product catalog, or designer brochure, we've recognized their need to produce beautiful marketing materials at high volumes. Since working with Progress Lighting, they've been recognized in the MarCom Awards for outstanding communication and design, winning Platinum for the Jeffrey Alan Marks Catalog and Gold for Experience Progress Magazine.

The flagship catalog of all their products. The conceptually drives the look and feel of all other peripheral marketing materials. This is still a valuable sales tool and the fastest way to peruse all the products.
Design Series - Jeffrey Alan Marks Collection

A fitting collaboration between dreamer and maker. Jeffrey's purposeful and authentic spaces beg to be lived in rather than existing only to be stylish. Today this Elle Décor A-List designer's spirited aesthetic is realized beyond his fortunate clients through licensing partnerships and a broader audience can look forward to homes that are more genuine, more personal and more engaging.
Multiple volumes per year of a magazine that showcases product, installations, designers and trends within the residential lighting industry.
A vision to give the new headquarters the feel of an inviting stylish hotel.
The design aesthetic of the Oprah Winfrey Network HQ can be described as "boutique hotel meets corporate office."

Environmental graphics were constructed with natural materials and unique touches. We designed the signage system to complement that aesthetic and in some cases blend into walls discretely. We chose the same oak used for the flooring and furniture, while balancing that warmth with a contemporary matte finish clear acrylic. The acrylic is back painted to match the wall color (hiding any unsightly mounting hardware). We collaborated with the Looking design studio, who oversaw the creative direction of the signage and HLW International, who led the overall interior design efforts.
We work hard and smart to efficiently deliver excellence to our clients.
With client's and executives flying in, the signs were paired with a vinyl printing system that OWN can manage, allowing them to be ready for any guest who may arrive with a matching nameplate.
high end furniture in a stone room
Creating high-end custom furniture for the hospitality market and winning, a proverbial David among Goliaths
Manufacturing Fine Furnishings for the world's most exceptional hotels and resorts since 2006.

Composition hangs its hat on Innovative Service + Resourceful Solutions. They've earned a reputation for the highest level of service in the industry, and have worked with some of the most talented craftsmen, artisans, mills, shops, factories and vendors around the globe. Their mission is to provide superior products that are a perfect fit for the composition of each clients installation. 

The idea of endless combinations led us to create a semi-fluid identity, based on the arrangement of the three "O's" from the word composition. The logo, like the service is simple, flexible and versatile with the ability to create endless compositions.
A fluid identity that is able to adapt to the context of its use.
Well-thought creativity that tells a story.
Composition's logo evolved into a mark that told the company's story and served as a driving element for future applications.
We created a system of wood and metal finish samples that appeal to designers, and prioritize usability.
A dental practice infused with beach culture to put people at ease - and only occasionally with laughing gas.
Sharing the sunshine of Southern California, we worked with our neighbors at Beachfront Dentistry to reinvigorate their brand to reflect their relaxed and casual personality.

Beachfront Dentistry is a modern practice located in Southern California who takes pride in getting to know their patients individually and customizing a plan that allows for long-term dental health. To communicate their effort to make their patients journey to a perfect smile easy and relaxed, we created a brand identity to match their professional yet casual personality.
Situated in Redondo Beach, the logo developed for Beachfront Dentistry uses the iconic lifeguard tower to reflect the easy Southern California vibe.
We create boldness and confidence for our clients.
With a fresh look, Beachfront Dentistry is ready to reach out to new and prospective patients, and connecting with their community.

A true neighborhood coffee shop owned by a husband and wife, creating space for meaningful connections.
There is a delicate balance to create a brand that epitomizes warmth, neighborhood and hip.

All the while harnessing the strengths of the husband and wife team that own the Blue Butterfly Coffee Co. on Main Street in El Segundo, CA. A study of opposites that, when combined, create something familiar, beautiful and inviting.
The double B logo, is both masculine and feminine, and has become an iconic stamp on Main Street El Segundo.
upside down branded blue butterfly mugs
With a new brand identity, Blue Butterfly became a warm and inviting locale for the community to enjoy their morning coffee, or afternoon pick-me-up.
front on image of a car between buildings
A media company wanting everyone to know they saw the world as an opportunity.

We worked with a great team at Innocean Worldwide USA, to create a visionary brand identity for CANVAS Worldwide, from scratch, stand-alone global media agency. CANVAS is a joint venture between INNOCEAN Worldwide and Horizon Media.

CANVAS represents the ability to generate custom-designed solutions for the ever-changing advertising landscape and needed to embody a spirit of creativity and curiosity. The company's philosophy is that given a blank canvas they can use imagination to achieve almost anything. We want people to see the brand as a fresh, creative, innovative new player. People should feel intrigued inspired, and curious to explore.

We wanted to create a brand that is meaningful, differentiated and flexible. So we explored wordmarks, symbols and numerous combinations that pushed the viewer to think and interact. Ideas ranged from broad brush strokes that would take on the content of the media clients to an abstract C that would be a portal to new media ideas and solutions.

The best solution, which was internally dubbed as "the implied frame," embodied the brand and its values in the most complete way. Meant to be less commercial and more avant garde or fashion-forward. This idea is based on an abstract frame that contains any and all possible content, which creates the implied canvas. The frame itself is derived from the cross strokes of the "A" letterforms. The final result is a minimal solution that invites the viewer to engage with the brand.

The first round of conceptual ideas was met with a standing ovation from the client's leadership. Approval of creative concepts have come quickly and the client is excited to showcase the brand in the launch of CANVAS and during the hiring process. Forthcoming CANVAS Worldwide launch anticipated for January 01, 2016. All the samples below are due to the great collaboration from the team at Innocean USA: 

Creative direction/writing - Chris Halas 
Associate Creative Director - Tricia Ting 
Art Director - Eric Stevens 
Website User Experience - Damien Dalli, Jon Farjo
Sample screens from the brand guidance document.
We create, value, and celebrate good design.
The Canvas logo's "implied frame" became a leading element in the entire system, creating a continuous and consistent personality in the design.
An important aspect of vision for Canvas was that it could translate as a high-end lifestyle brand. Here is a select collection of those executions.
Chief Executive Office of LA County
The “buck stops here” with a branding system for Los Angeles County's CEO

The CEO’s office carries out the business of the county and initiatives set forth by an elected Board of Supervisors. They required a brand that communicated confidently as a public department with the operational complexity, scope and budget that exceed many corporations.

Reddoor went through an extensive RFP process and was ultimately selected by the County to design a logo and develop a system that could help bring consistency across the large organization. As we dug into public stats about the County of Los Angeles we discovered the staggering size of the county and its infrastructure. You can see by the data below (as of 2023-2024), the County of Los Angeles truly rivals the size of states and even some countries:

Here's the text from the Los Angeles County infographic: County of Los Angeles POPULATION 9,539,730 Serves more residents than 40 States BUDGETED POSITIONS 115,324 48th largest employer if fortune 500 company ADOPTED BUDGET FOR 2023-24 $46.7 B Larger budget than 35 states ALONGSIDE THE Top 20 countries in the world for GDP

The logo was born out of a modern monogram/seal concept. It needed to hold its own next to the LA County seal while also living in harmony with it. We wanted to communicate the dual nature of being a public department operating at the scale of a major corporation. The brand's boldness is meant to stand confidently beside any Fortune 100 company.

“The buck stops here.” was a phrase our team used internally when discussing how the Chief Executive Office is uniquely positioned in the County's overall structure. They are the final authority on budget recommendations before they are submitted to the Board of Supervisors. Design decisions led us to a visually distinct and strong red period signifying the “final word.”
ceo logo for all different departments
These guidelines are extensive, not only showing logo usage, color palettes and collateral examples but also giving guidance for department and initiative application of the brand.

The CEO is in charge of implementing the Board of Supervisors' ambitious policy agenda. We needed a visual system that could help give the initiatives efficient and professional branding out of the gate.
other initiatives using the branding scheme

Since the CEO's office communicates with a wide range of internal and external audiences about the budget and other key priorities, a customized set of icons was developed to provide maximum clarity and flexibility.

LoneHollow Ranch (LHR) is a beautiful, Texas ranch and retreat property operated by Young Life in Utopia, Texas. Our engagement with LHR began as a consultation on how to position the ranch as a possible venue for retreats and celebrations.

LHR is occupied most of the summer and sporadically throughout the rest of the year, but goal was to position LoneHollow as a destination for student, adult and school retreats or even for weddings, big gatherings or a remote adventure camp. LoneHollow Ranch has it all and can accommodate groups from 50-500.
“Welcome to your home away from home.”
The headquarters building at LHR welcomes you with it’s distinctive pillars, which serves as inspiration in the letterform shape for the ranch’s logotype.
A galvanized on corten steel signage system was designed to help guests find their way around the sprawling ranch.
We created an easy to use, illustrated map to make simple sense of the the massive property. Simiplification and ease of use were the priority.

We developed a series of brochures and digital presentations that spoke directly to the specific needs of different customer types: Leadership Retreats, Celebrations, Adult Retreats and Student Retreats.

A testament to the transformational power of the place. LHR welcomes groups to have one of the most meaningful weekends or weeks of their lives. It was important to feature the beauty of the place along with the amenities and the flexibility that the ranch offers to serve a diverse customer base.
parachuting pair of kids having a great time
Reddoor partnered with Young Life to create a Salesforce-based compliance management system for employees and volunteers worldwide.

Backend software development and integration was completed by Young Life. The YL Connect tool is a decade(s) overdue update to managing thousands of employees and volunteers. Everything from training, to background checks to on-going education and compliance. Our goal was to create an experience that made the required and mundane tasks enjoyable and as easy as possible to accomplish.
Specific pages, shown in mobile format that a typical user would experience in the process of using the app.

We took every opportunity we could to infuse the trademark life-giving enthusiasm that Young Life is known for. We used candid pictures of student, staff and volunteers throughout the experience to emphasize the human element and importance of the mission of Young Life.
Reddoor partnered with Young Life to create a Salesforce-based compliance management system for employees and volunteers worldwide.

1-800-DENTIST is a fixture in the dental space, with over 25,000 dentists in their referral network. We created a compelling website to guide patients towards making the best decision for their dental health.

Helping the LA County Counsel modernize the brand of an office that has already been modernized.

macbook with smiling person brand in the corner

Captivate approached us with a unification problem. They had two Texas locations in with different types of clients, different office cultures and different ways of doing business. The first task was a naming simplification effort. We combined Sterling Home Technologies and Captive Audio to make Captivate. There was a desire to move away from Captive, which could have negative connotations. This move was as much for the internal team culture as it was for the customer.

Following the rename, we went to work on the brand. The goal was to create an elevated brand that had meaning. We utilized actual sound recordings of the word "captivate" to create a sound wave element, and used an RGB color gradient, each alluding to the immersive experience that high-end audio-visual equipment can deliver.

When considering the vehicle design, our ultimate litmus test was "are these vehicle graphics going to be distinctive and intriguing if a service vehicle is sitting in the driveway of a multi-million dollar house?"

For each new brand we create, we also create a one-page "Cheat Sheet" that helps with client with simple reminders on how to consistently communicate with the new brand. Obviously, this isn't exhaustive, but a great reference guide for client-created design.
This simple, yet impressive, metal card is carried by the service team and left with clients so that it's easy to schedule and request service.

The AURA Optical Gas Imaging (OGI) camera was created in partnership with Sherpa Design for ChampionX. The AURA camera is the best-in-class optical gas imaging camera. It's primary function is to detect methane in oil field operations. Methane detection is very important to energy companies, first, because it is harmful to the environment and second because there are stringent regulations and penalties for releasing methane into the atmosphere.
Our effort included naming, branding and collaboration on the product design of the AURA OGI camera.

The triangular "A's" reference the angular geometry of the camera body. The yellow and black cerakote along with rugged design elements give the camera what it needs to stand the punishment of an oilfield operational environment with high temps, severe dust and rigorous use.

The cameras and technological capability are industry leading. These are also very expensive, so each one was delivered in custom AURA NANUK cases with bespoke foam inserts for batteries, tablets and necessary accessories.

Boerne Little League (BLL) was looking to upgrade their basic little league look in order to attract families and players to their hometown baseball and softball programs. We began the project by focusing on the baseball caps. We wanted to create a hat that kids would WANT to wear at school. Little league, at least in Texas, is up against numerous club and travel team options for baseball. So, we wanted to brand BLL in a modern and compelling way as a way of saying you can still play competitive baseball and softball without having to sacrifice your entire weekend driving across the state.
We developed an entire system of logos that were applied across player jerseys, coach jerseys and merchandise for the families and fans.
dark and light branding with hat and shirt

In 2024 the Boerne Little League All Stars went all the way to the Little League World Series and finished fourth after losing to Venezuela in the third place game. Pictured here are some of that 2024 team. Way to go Boerne!
Local News Coverage of the Boerne All Stars

As an established force in the analytics industry, Alteryx wanted a headquarters that represented their global impact. Through carefully curated graphics, we designed signage for each themed level of the Irvine HQ that represented the various countries of their regional offices. This translated from jewel box meeting rooms to custom workspace signs, following the same linear theme with individual personalities and colors to distinguish each floor.

From the niche vintage vehicle to the mud-tracked floors of a soccer van, anyone with a car deserves to experience full service from head to tail-lights. Enzo's wanted a brand that reflects their obsession to detail when it comes to detailing cars. We dove into an involved process, cruising from their logo, to website, and the space to help make the experience as detailed as a wash.
obsessive clean over image of headlight

For Enzo's they had a vision for who they wanted to be, but needed that extra bit of care with the detailing. We refined their logo to feel reminiscent of vintage car shops, while feeling modern and clean.
four panels: 100% hand wash, enzos logo one, enzos logo 2, quality guarunteed
After developing a brand, we provided recommendations on all branded areas of Enzo's to create a consistent brand look. This included how their brand appeared on uniforms, merch, and products.
In addition, the website is in development to provide potential customers with information and peace-of-mind that they are working with experts in car detailing.

The final frontier of the brand Is the social media presence. We made recommendations for their existing instagram page, and provided direction for campaign concepts that are easy to execute and maintain.

As an added value to the experience, Enzo's will also have a cafe on-site for customers to have a pleasurable place to wait while their car gets cleaned. We gave recommendations on their brand's look and feel, from cafe branded materials to interior finishes.
Being situated in an heavily trafficked intersection in Boise, Idaho, it was important to create an impact with the signage on the street corner and building facade.

We partnered Gallery Sonder to build a brand representative of their immersive, community-oriented space for artists and enthusiasts. Our collaborative effort resulted in a beautiful and bold design that invites you into the unique narrative of each individual artist.
big geometric sculpture and wall art
The brand is developed around the concept of a portal that allows for a peek into the art, but expands into an immersive experience.
extended versions of each letter in both directions
all the artist names with their art put in an extended letter
We approached all branded materials with the intention of creating a feeling of intrigue, through keyholes, and immersion, through expanding and revealing elements.
To focus on the artists and their forthcoming show, invitations represent a peek into the artwork, showing a cropped and detailed portion of a piece.
To complete the experience, we created signage engaging passersby on foot and by car.
After much anticipation, Gallery Sonder celebrated its opening night November 2024. The gallery will be a showcase for fresh, up and coming talent.
putting while someone else holds a branded flag

After a life-changing experiencing at a Youth Peace Camp in Serbia, founder Shane Hanson knew his purpose was to create a non-profit that served Foster Youth through sports, mentorship, and training. Due to the deeply flawed foster care system,  many children who need safe and secure environments are left with a lack of structure and resources. Freedom Youth Foundation was created to help fill that gap along side a community of amazing volunteers and sponsors. They care for thousands of foster kids each year in California, Idaho and Mexico.
With a brand that has good standing within the communities they serve, it was important to honor the intent of FYF's original logo.
Once a concise look and feel was created, we quickly developed all collateral for their day-to-day operations, marketing, and events.
In a recent fundraising event, we donated our services to create branded and marketing materials for a golf tournament.
branded signs for each golf hole
hero image of putting with flag

Medical Solutions of Texas is a Service Disabled Veteran Owned Business (SDVOSB) that acts as the bridge between medical supply companies and the Department of Defense and VA hospitals. They have the connections and experience to help private companies sell their medical suplies and solutions to the government.

MSOT's lacked a brand, identity and website that looked legitimate in the eyes of the medical community or the military end user. So, we developed a brand that was inspired by stenciled and angular military lettering and logistics shipping containers. The brand uses a color palette sampled from camoflauge and carries with it that tactical "we get it done" mentality.
The lettering is distinctive, simple and no nonsense.

The MSOT website is a place for medical supply sales reps to understand how to sell to the government. Pages help the view understand how MSOT helps clarify a complex process. We featured "end users." The ultimate recipients of the  medical care offered and featured them over the lifetime of military service from new recruits, to growing families all the way up to retired and aging combat veterans.
Our websites are always designed and programmed with desktop, laptop, tablet and mobile experiences in mind.

We've enjoyed a lengthy partnership with SummitTrek and consider it an honor to create tools for their coaching practice that bring lasting impact in the lives and businesses of their clients. We help communicate on their behalf by way of brand re-design, monthly workbooks, email newsletters, assorted print materials and websites. Summit is in the business of transforming and restoring lives and businesses to their original intended purpose and we love helping them do that.

Summit makes use of carefully curated quotes throughout their materials. When we recently re-designed their brand we made sure to identify a font that felt authentically hand written, to give the personal touch whenever short quotes are displayed.

We've created a comprehensive system of tools, worksheets, workbooks all with a simple minimalistic aesthetic. We never want the design to get in the way of the process or the meaningful exercises the user is experiencing.
One of the most impactful Summit offerings is the Life Planning Retreat. The weekend is an opportunity to Rekindle Your Passions. Discover Your Purpose and Live Differently.
our process is found in restoring leaders and organizations to their original intened purpose through coaching
With a common vision, we developed a brand that is reflective of Revogen's innovative and high-quality products that help patients heal.
